summary: Iter 60 backend + frontend tested. 14/15 pytest pass (93%). ONE CRITICAL BACKEND BUG found: Portal automation background task is never awaited due to malformed background_tasks.add_task(asyncio.create_task, _run()) — job stays in 'queued' status forever. All other iter60 flows work: bulk-correction fields/dry-run/real/group-cascade/403, bulk-import (create+update+auto-masters), attendance-email config email filtering, dry-run trigger, FY filter for salary/compliance runs, sub_admin READ access (salary-runs, salary detail, export.csv, register.pdf, compliance ecr.txt, bonus report.xlsx). Frontend: all 4 new routes (/bulk-employee-correction, /attendance-email, /portal-automation, /reports) render without red-screens; role-gate messages appear correctly for unauthenticated sessions. AdminWebShell contains all 4 new nav entries in the super-admin section.

critical.issue: Portal automation background task is never scheduled/awaited. Line 900 of /app/backend/utils/iter60_features.py: `background_tasks.add_task(asyncio.create_task, _run())` — this eagerly constructs the coroutine `_run()` but wraps it in `asyncio.create_task` as a delayed call, so it emits RuntimeWarning 'coroutine _run was never awaited' and the job stays in 'queued' status forever. Fix: `background_tasks.add_task(_run)` (pass the async function directly, FastAPI's BackgroundTasks awaits it). Verified in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log: 'coroutine register_iter60_features.<locals>.create_portal_automation_job.<locals>._run was never awaited'.

action_items: Fix portal automation background task scheduling in /app/backend/utils/iter60_features.py line ~900. Change `background_tasks.add_task(asyncio.create_task, _run())` to `background_tasks.add_task(_run)`. Then verify a queued job progresses to running/completed_login/paused_captcha/failed within 20s.
critical_code_review_comments: iter60_features.py:900 — improper BackgroundTasks usage causes portal automation coroutine to never run (RuntimeWarning in server logs).
The `/admin/employees/bulk-correction` uses `require_role(admin, ['super_admin', 'sub_admin'])` correctly — company_admin gets 403 as expected.
Attendance email config correctly filters invalid entries via `[e.strip().lower() for e in recipients if e and '@' in e]`.
Frontend gate strings are user-friendly ('Only Super/Sub-admins can bulk correct employees.' etc.) — no red-screens on any of 4 new routes.
seed_data_creation: Created test employees via bulk-import, test masters (groups/depts/designations) via bulk-correction cascade, and one sub_admin (email qa.sub.*@test.local) via /admin/sub-admins. All prefixed for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Portal automation bug fix requires simple one-line change. After fix, re-run pytest backend/tests/test_iter60_features.py::TestPortalAutomation::test_create_and_poll — should progress from queued to running/failed within 20s. Note: sub_admin creation requires 'password' field on POST /admin/sub-admins; OTP login as that email then works. Employees are fetched via /api/admin/employees?company_id=... which returns {employees:[...]}. Salary/compliance runs return {runs:[...]}. Masters return {items:[...]}.
rca_of_issue: Portal automation queue-stuck bug — Root cause: `background_tasks.add_task(asyncio.create_task, _run())` passes `asyncio.create_task` as the sync callable and `_run()` (already-called coroutine) as an argument. FastAPI's BackgroundTasks runs sync callables in a threadpool (no running event loop), so `asyncio.create_task(coro)` inside a threadpool raises RuntimeError silently (get_running_loop fails), leaving the coroutine unscheduled and un-awaited. Mitigation: pass the coroutine function directly — `background_tasks.add_task(_run)` — since `_run` is an async function, FastAPI will properly await it inside the event loop after the response is sent.
